raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need find files named job.history under /testbed, grep for FAIL, count. Use find and grep -l and wc -l. Command: find /testbed -type f -name 'job.history' -exec grep -q 'FAIL' {} \; -print | wc -l. But need count only those that match. Use -exec grep -q 'FAIL' {} \; -print. Then pipe to wc -l. So final: find /testbed -type f -name 'job.history' -exec grep -q 'FAIL' {} \; -print | wc -l.

So final: find /testbed -type f -name 'job.history' -exec grep -q 'FAIL' {} \; -print | wc -l.

task.query: Count the number of files named 'job.history' under '/testbed' directory tree that match 'FAIL' in their contents
task.gold: find /testbed -name job.history | xargs grep -l FAIL | wc -l
